localCopies.label: Pinned CME repository LICENSE
notes: Complete supporting rights document; it contains no target text.

rights.basis: The pinned repository LICENSE expressly dedicates software, including source and binary code, but does not clearly state that historical XML, OCR, editorial markup, or data are covered. Public-domain status of the medieval texts and old printed editions does not by itself settle rights in this digital encoding layer.
rights.component: Pinned Kyvero-Press/CME XML, OCR, and markup layer

entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2638
notes: The 1,046,959-byte XML contains 5 DIV1, 272 DIV2, 4 HEAD, 22,672 L, 670 LG, 1,033 MILESTONE, 6 P, 694 PB, and 663 NOTE1 elements.
The source header declares 694 linked 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images, but those U-M page images are not repository files.

rights.basis: The pinned repository LICENSE expressly dedicates software, including source and binary code, but does not clearly state that historical XML, OCR, editorial markup, or data are covered. bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, EETS Extra Series 42, 49, 59 (London, 1883, 1887, 1891), composite title-quarter sheet issued in 1905; Furnivall's explanatory statement dated 25 November 1905.
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., Limited
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 42, 49, 59
bibliographic.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version

dateStatements.label: title-quarter-sheet issue
dateStatements.value: Issued in 1905; Furnivall's explanatory statement dated 25 November 1905

entities.description: The later binding presentation immediately underlying the U-M encoding: a title-quarter sheet issued because members wished to bind the three previously published parts together. It is a carrier-level compilation, not a fourth publication of a single newly edited text.
entities.label: Zupitza Guy of Warwick first-version composite with 1905 title-quarter sheet

rights.basis: All identified print components were published no later than 1905; the medieval texts are centuries older. This does not license modern scans, OCR, catalog prose, or manuscript photography.
rights.component: Underlying 1840, 1875-91, and 1905 printed texts and medieval source texts

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part I, EETS Extra Series 42 (London, 1883; temporary notice 1885).

dateStatements.label: publication
dateStatements.value: 1883 title date; temporary notice dated 21 February 1885

entities.description: The first separately issued parallel-text part, printed pp. 1-224. Its temporary notice is dated Berlin, 21 February 1885 despite the title's 1883 series date.
entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part I (1883; notice dated 1885)

rights.basis: This edition part was published in 1883. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.
rights.component: Underlying 1883 Part I edition text and editorial apparatus

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part II, EETS Extra Series 49 (London, 1887).

entities.description: The second separately issued parallel-text part, continuing printed pp. 225-383.
entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part II (1887)

rights.basis: This edition part was published in 1887. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.
rights.component: Underlying 1887 Part II edition text and editorial apparatus

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part III, EETS Extra Series 59 (London, 1891).

entities.description: The third separately issued part, printed pp. 384-674, completing the parallel Guy streams and adding Reinbrun with an Anglo-Norman ending supply.
entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part III (1891)

rights.basis: This edition part was published in 1891. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.
rights.component: Underlying 1891 Part III edition text and editorial apparatus

bibliographic.citation: William B. D. D. Turnbull, ed., The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1840).
bibliographic.title: The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son

entities.description: The Abbotsford Club edition of the Auchinleck couplet Guy, stanzaic Guy, and Reinbrun texts. It is Zupitza's immediate printed intermediary for those streams, subsequently corrected and collated against the manuscript.
entities.label: Turnbull, The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son (1840)

rights.basis: Turnbull's edition was published in 1840. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.
rights.component: Underlying 1840 edition text and editorial apparatus

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The Second or 15th-Century Version, EETS Extra Series 25-26 (London, 1875-76).
bibliographic.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The Second or 15th-Century Version

entities.description: Zupitza's separate complete edition of the later couplet version from CUL Ff.2.38. It is supporting evidence for the exact source lines 9234-36 inserted into the aha2638 Caius stream, not the global source edition of aha2638.
entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick second or fifteenth-century version (1875-76)

rights.basis: This edition was published in 1875-76. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.
rights.component: Underlying 1875-76 second-version edition text and editorial apparatus

entities.description: The conceptual first Middle English couplet redaction, conventionally 11,095 lines across its witnesses.
entities.label: Guy of Warwick, first couplet version (DIMEV 4907 / IMEV 3145)

rights.basis: The conceptual work is medieval and the evidenced textual tradition predates modern copyright terms. This United States conclusion does not cover editions, encodings, catalog prose, or images, and no United Kingdom conclusion is asserted.
rights.component: Underlying medieval first-couplet Guy work

entities.description: The conceptual 11,976-line later Middle English couplet redaction, complete in CUL Ff.2.38 and partially spliced into Caius 107/176.
entities.label: Guy of Warwick, later couplet version (DIMEV 4908 / IMEV 3146)

rights.basis: The conceptual work is medieval and the evidenced textual tradition predates modern copyright terms. This United States conclusion does not cover editions, encodings, catalog prose, or images, and no United Kingdom conclusion is asserted.
rights.component: Underlying medieval later-couplet Guy work

entities.description: The independent 299-stanza Auchinleck account of Guy after marriage.
entities.label: Stanzaic Guy of Warwick after marriage (DIMEV 1557 / IMEV 946)

rights.basis: The conceptual work is medieval and the evidenced textual tradition predates modern copyright terms. This United States conclusion does not cover editions, encodings, catalog prose, or images, and no United Kingdom conclusion is asserted.
rights.component: Underlying medieval stanzaic Guy work

entities.description: The independent 127-stanza Middle English Reinbrun romance, surviving imperfectly in the Auchinleck manuscript.
entities.label: Reinbrun, son of Guy of Warwick (DIMEV 2907 / IMEV 1754)

rights.basis: The conceptual work is medieval and the evidenced textual tradition predates modern copyright terms. This United States conclusion does not cover editions, encodings, catalog prose, or images, and no United Kingdom conclusion is asserted.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Reinbrun work

entities.description: The wider Anglo-Norman romance tradition represented by Parker MS 50 and Harley MS 3775. Exact Parker passages repair the defective opening and ending in Zupitza's edition; no one surviving codex is asserted as the universal source of all Middle English redactions.
entities.label: Gui de Warewic, Anglo-Norman romance tradition

rights.basis: The conceptual work is medieval and the evidenced textual tradition predates modern copyright terms. This United States conclusion does not cover editions, encodings, catalog prose, or images, and no United Kingdom conclusion is asserted.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic work

entities.description: The principal physical witness for three distinct target works: first-couplet Guy at ff. 108ra-146vb, stanzaic Guy at ff. 146vb-167rb, and imperfect Reinbrun at ff. 167rb-175vb.

entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century parchment codex containing Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic. Zupitza directly copies its ff. 103ra-va for the defective Auchinleck opening and f. 181ra-rb for the defective Reinbrun ending.
entities.label: Cambridge, Corpus Christi College, Parker MS 50

physicalDescription.extent: 181 folios plus 8 preliminary or additional leaves

access.accessMethod: Arrange an appointment with the College Library and justify scholarly need.
access.entityLabel: Gonville and Caius College MS 107/176

entities.description: The fifteenth-century composite couplet Guy witness transcribed in Zupitza's parallel stream at manuscript pp. 1-271. Modern analysis identifies it as a splice of first- and later-couplet redactions.
entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 107/176

notes: James records donation by William Moore.

rights.basis: The College copyright policy says supplied material is normally for private research and requires written application, purpose and format, and acceptance of item-specific publication terms or fees.

entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century paper codex containing the complete 11,976-line later-couplet Guy at ff. 161r-231r. Only lines 9234-36 are directly inserted into aha2638, replacing three lines absent from Caius.

next_steps: Ask DIMEV whether the citation means global edition coverage, a lost apparatus reference, or bibliographic association; do not create a direct Sloane-to-aha2638 edge without page-level evidence.
openQuestions.question: Why does DIMEV record 4907 associate Zupitza 1883-91 with the Sloane MS 1044 fragment when neither the inspected aha2638 XML nor the three target part OCR files names Sloane 1044?

next_steps: Ask library@cai.cam.ac.uk for existing photography or a whole-object quote and written research/publication terms; identify pp.1-271 separately after delivery.
openQuestions.question: Can Gonville and Caius College provide a complete color surrogate of MS 107/176, including all 136 folios, bindings, flyleaves, and blanks, and what reuse terms would apply?

next_steps: Request the whole physical object through CUL Imaging Services, not only Guy ff.161r-231r or lines 9234-36; separately verify the 1979 monochrome facsimile's availability.
openQuestions.question: Can Cambridge University Library provide a complete color surrogate of MS Ff.2.38 and exact item-level reuse terms?

next_steps: Monitor the exact catalog and ask the British Library about f.15 and whole-object delivery; retain the relation as Zupitza's historical collation claim until the page can be inspected directly.
openQuestions.question: When will digitized content for Harley MS 3775 be restored, or can the British Library provide a mediated complete-object surrogate?

next_steps: Retry in an interactive browser or ask U-M Digital Content and Collections; do not equate historical PB references with confirmed current downloadability.
openQuestions.question: What current U-M route exposes the 694 linked page images and an item-level rights statement without the Cloudflare challenge?

reviewNotes: The edition title's singular 'version' is historical naming; the manifest preserves four medieval work/redaction identities and passage-scoped supplies.
The couplet-to-stanzaic boundary is shared on Auchinleck f. 146vb; the stanzaic-to-Reinbrun boundary is shared on f. 167rb.
Sloane MS 1044 is not given a direct target relation: it appears in Zupitza's separate 1875-76 apparatus and DIMEV's first-version census, but not in the inspected aha2638 XML or 1883-91 part OCR.
Negative online-facsimile and access findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-16.

lineage.summary: The checked-in aha2638 XML copies University of Michigan CME item AHA2638.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding of a carrier assembled under a title-quarter sheet dated 25 November 1905 from Julius Zupitza's separately issued EETS parts of 1883, 1887, and 1891. The parallel edition combines several distinct textual streams: the Auchinleck first-couplet Guy, the Caius 107/176 composite couplet witness, the independent Auchinleck stanzaic Guy after marriage, and the Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation. Zupitza inherited Turnbull's 1840 text for the Auchinleck streams but used J. A. H. Murray's manuscript collation and his own corrections; Caius was transcribed directly. The defective Auchinleck opening is supplied from Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic in Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50 and collated against Harley MS 3775 f. 15; the defective Reinbrun ending is supplied from Parker MS 50 f. 181r. Three Caius lines at printed p. 531 are supplied from CUL Ff.2.38 lines 9234-36. Complete NLS and Parker manuscript surrogates and complete scans/OCR of all three part issues, Turnbull's prior edition, Zupitza's 1875-76 Ff.2.38 edition, DIMEV data, and supporting Caius catalog/access evidence are cached with exact target-presence declarations. Physical, textual, editorial, scan, OCR, access, and rights layers remain separate.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous authors of the distinct Middle English Guy of Warwick and Reinbrun redactions
catalogSummary.date: Constituent Middle English redactions conventionally circa 1300; EETS parts published 1883, 1887, and 1891; composite title-quarter-sheet issued 1905
catalogSummary.editor: Julius Zupitza, with J. A. H. Murray's Auchinleck collation and earlier text by William B. D. D. Turnbull
catalogSummary.region: England; London is a questioned composition localization and must remain tentative
catalogSummary.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version

catalogingSubject.label: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloging subject is the edition-defined composite represented by University of Michigan item AHA2638.0001.001: Julius Zupitza's parallel presentation of the first Middle English couplet Guy redaction in the Auchinleck and Caius witnesses, the independent Auchinleck stanzaic continuation after Guy's marriage, and the Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation, together with short Anglo-Norman passages inserted where the Auchinleck texts are defective and a three-line Cambridge University Library Ff.2.38 supply in the Caius stream. It is not one homogeneous medieval work, every Guy of Warwick redaction or witness, the whole Auchinleck codex, any other physical manuscript, Turnbull's 1840 edition, Zupitza's separate 1875-76 second-version edition, the three separately issued EETS parts, the 1905 title-quarter-sheet carrier, the U-M encoding and images, or the repository XML.

contentParts.label: 1905 composite title-quarter-sheet and local editorial apparatus

locators.value: title-quarter-sheet and verso note dated 25 Nov. 1905

notes: Editorial prose and title matter are not counted as a fifth medieval work.
contentParts.summary: Composite title matter, Furnivall's signed explanation for binding the three separately issued parts, section headings, source notices, manuscript milestones, and textual notes retained in the encoding. The half-title and back matter are omitted.

contentParts.label: Auchinleck couplet Guy of Warwick, with supplied Anglo-Norman opening

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[1]/DIV2[1-128]; 7,416 L; opening at printed p. 2; English begins at milestone 108a:1; ends p. 384

notes: The 7,416 XML L count includes the bracketed Anglo-Norman repair and must not be substituted for DIMEV's conventional 11,095-line extent of the first couplet version across witnesses.
contentParts.summary: The first Guy redaction before marriage as preserved imperfectly in the Auchinleck manuscript, preceded in the edition by an Anglo-Norman opening copied from Parker MS 50 and collated against Harley MS 3775. The English witness runs from f. 108ra through f. 146vb and ends at conventional line 7306 with Guy's dragon victory.

contentParts.label: Caius Guy of Warwick composite couplet witness

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[2]; 10,116 L in 115 LG; printed odd pages 3-629; terminal page break [630]
odd-page Caius stream pp. 385-629; three-line supply p. 531, lines 9515-17
lines 9234-36 supply the three edition lines at p. 531

notes: The three-line Ff.2.38 passage is a direct, passage-scoped source; the complete Ff.2.38 later-couplet work is not otherwise flattened into the Caius witness.
contentParts.summary: The parallel Caius MS 107/176 text on manuscript pp. 1-271. The witness combines the first couplet redaction with two substantial portions of the later couplet redaction; Zupitza also inserts three lines absent from Caius from Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, lines 9234-36, at printed p. 531 / edition lines 9515-17.

contentParts.label: Auchinleck stanzaic Guy of Warwick after marriage

locators.value: p. 384 after couplet line 7306 through p. 630

notes: The XML's 3,582 L elements do not equal 299 multiplied by twelve because representation and printed lineation differ; DIMEV's structural description remains 299 twelve-line stanzas.
contentParts.summary: An independent 299-stanza tail-rhyme continuation of Guy's story after marriage, beginning on the same f. 146vb and printed p. 384 as the end of the couplet text and ending at f. 167rb / printed p. 630 with 'Amen par charite.'

contentParts.label: Reinbrun, son of Guy of Warwick, with supplied Anglo-Norman ending

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[3]; printed pp. 631-674; 1,558 L in 128 LG; final LG is the 14-line Anglo-Norman supply
ff. 167rb-175vb; Middle English ends imperfectly 'Inte Ingelonde þai gonne saile'

contentParts.summary: The Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation, 127 Middle English twelve-line stanzas from f. 167rb to its imperfect end on f. 175vb, followed in Zupitza by a bracketed Anglo-Norman continuation copied from Parker MS 50 f. 181ra-rb.

dateStatements.display: The constituent Middle English Guy of Warwick redactions are conventionally dated circa 1300
notes: This broad work-date statement is distinct from the 1331-40 Auchinleck production date and the fifteenth-century Caius and Ff.2.38 copies.

dateStatements.display: The immediate Zupitza edition was published in three EETS parts dated 1883, 1887, and 1891
notes: This is the publication layer immediately underlying the U-M encoding, not the first publication of all constituent works; Turnbull had printed the Auchinleck streams in 1840.

dateStatements.display: The title-quarter-sheet for binding the three parts together was issued in 1905; Furnivall's explanatory statement is dated 25 November 1905

extent.description: Four medieval textual streams plus an editorial frame. The XML contains 22,672 L in 670 LG, 272 DIV2, 5 DIV1 overall, 663 NOTE1, 1,033 MILESTONE, 6 P, and 694 PB. The Auchinleck Guy DIV1 divides into 7,416 L / 128 LG for the couplet-and-opening-supply section and 3,582 L / 299 LG for the stanzaic continuation; Caius has 10,116 L / 115 LG; Reinbrun and its supplied ending have 1,558 L / 128 LG. These are representation metrics, not normalized critical line counts: DIMEV separately describes 11,095 couplet lines, 299 twelve-line stanzas, 127 twelve-line Reinbrun stanzas, and the 11,976-line later-couplet work partly spliced into Caius.

formStatements.description: Verse romance streams with a prose editorial frame
Short rhyming couplets, with Caius splicing portions of the first and later couplet redactions
Twelve-line tail-rhyme stanzas in the Middle English portions

scope.description: The Middle English stanzaic portions, excluding the Anglo-Norman Reinbrun repair

languageStatements.label: Anglo-Norman French
notes: These are edition-level repair passages, not evidence that the four Middle English streams form one bilingual medieval work.

scope.description: Opening supplied from Parker MS 50 and Reinbrun ending supplied from the same manuscript

notes: The edition's title 'first or fourteenth-century version' is retained as historical naming, not treated as proof that its four medieval streams are one version.
Shared folio boundaries are explicit: the couplet text ends and stanzaic Guy begins on f. 146vb; stanzaic Guy ends and Reinbrun begins on f. 167rb.
The CUL Ff.2.38 relation is limited to three copied lines. The Parker relations are limited to the opening and ending repairs. Harley 3775 is limited to collation of the opening repair.
The U-M language header says eng, but direct inspection establishes Middle English as primary, Anglo-Norman in inserted verse, and modern English in editorial matter.
The NLS contents page's stanzaic start typo of f. 145vb is not propagated; the official transcription, visible boundary, Zupitza, and DIMEV support f. 146vb.

next_steps: Review the scholarship underlying D-MER's question-marked place field separately for each redaction; do not infer composition from the Auchinleck codex's production place.
openQuestions.question: What evidence supports or refutes the Database of Middle English Romance's questioned London composition localization for the distinct Guy and Reinbrun redactions?

next_steps: Check a clean critical bibliography or consult DIMEV editors before asserting that Sloane 1044 contributes directly to aha2638; retain it only as a collateral first-version witness meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: Why does DIMEV record 4907 list Zupitza's 1883-91 edition for the Sloane MS 1044 fragment when the inspected target XML and three part OCR files expose no Sloane 1044 reading or source notice?

next_steps: Ask Gonville and Caius College and Cambridge University Library for whole-object research copies and terms; monitor the exact British Library Harley record while recovery continues.
openQuestions.question: Can complete public or mediated digital surrogates be obtained for Caius MS 107/176, CUL MS Ff.2.38, and Harley MS 3775?

assertion.notes: D-MER prints London with a question mark; the Auchinleck codex's London production does not independently prove composition there.

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous author of the first Middle English couplet Guy of Warwick
notes: Witness variation does not imply separate named authors; this responsibility remains distinct from the later-couplet, stanzaic, Reinbrun, and Anglo-Norman responsibilities.

scope.description: The first-couplet redaction as represented by Auchinleck and the first-version portions of Caius

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous author of the later Middle English couplet Guy of Warwick

scope.description: The two later-couplet portions spliced into Caius and the three-line Ff.2.38 repair

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous author of the stanzaic Guy of Warwick after marriage

scope.description: The 127-stanza Middle English Reinbrun text, excluding Zupitza's Anglo-Norman ending repair

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous author of the Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic repair passages
notes: This passage responsibility does not assign one author to the Middle English redactions or to every Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic version.

scope.description: Only the exact Anglo-Norman opening and ending passages inserted from Parker MS 50
Direct Caius transcription and manuscript-based correction, collation, and supplied passages for the other streams
Auchinleck collation and most Part I side-notes used by Zupitza
1840 prior edition retained as Zupitza's immediate textual intermediary for the Auchinleck streams
Signed 25 November 1905 explanation on the composite title-quarter-sheet

summaries.value: Zupitza's edition juxtaposes two Middle English couplet witnesses of Guy's career and then continues with the Auchinleck manuscript's independent 299-stanza account of Guy after marriage and its 127-stanza, imperfect Reinbrun narrative. The Auchinleck couplet stream begins with an Anglo-Norman repair from Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50, collated against Harley MS 3775; Reinbrun ends with another Parker MS 50 repair. The Caius stream itself splices first- and later-couplet redactions and receives three missing lines from CUL Ff.2.38. These passage-level repairs and shared narrative boundaries do not turn the edition into one medieval textual witness.

The U-M XML retains the 1905 title-quarter sheet and all printed text pages 1-674 but marks the half-title and back matter omitted. It contains three BODY/DIV1 streams: the combined Auchinleck couplet plus stanzaic Guy text, the parallel Caius text, and Reinbrun. The structural split between couplet and stanzaic Guy occurs within the first DIV1 at DIV2 129 and shared printed p. 384 / manuscript f. 146vb.

notes: This is an edition and carrier title, not an authorial medieval title for one unified work.
titles.supplied_by: Normalized from the 1905 composite title-quarter sheet
titles.value: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
Syth the tyme that Cryst Ihesu / Thorough hys grace and vertu
God graunt hem heuen blis to mede / Þat herken to mi romaunce rede
Iesu, þat ert of miȝte most / Fader, and sone, and holy gost

work.regionDisplay: England; London is a questioned composition localization and must remain tentative
work.summary: Zupitza's edition juxtaposes two Middle English couplet witnesses of Guy's career and then continues with the Auchinleck manuscript's independent 299-stanza account of Guy after marriage and its 127-stanza, imperfect Reinbrun narrative. The Auchinleck couplet stream begins with an Anglo-Norman repair from Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50, collated against Harley MS 3775; Reinbrun ends with another Parker MS 50 repair. The Caius stream itself splices first- and later-couplet redactions and receives three missing lines from CUL Ff.2.38. These passage-level repairs and shared narrative boundaries do not turn the edition into one medieval textual witness.
